info.title: I-Ching Oracle API
info.description:
I-Ching oracle API + MCP for AI agents and developers. Access 3,000 years of Chinese wisdom with all 64 hexagrams, 384 changing lines, 8 trigrams, and modern interpretations for love, career, and decision-making. Cast readings with the authentic three-coin method, get daily hexagrams, and explore the Book of Changes programmatically. 9+ endpoints optimized for divination apps, AI oracle chatbots, fortune-telling platforms, daily wisdom features, and spiritual guidance products.

# RoxyAPI: AI-Native Insight Infrastructure

> **Base URL:** `https://roxyapi.com/api/v2`
> All endpoint paths below are relative to this base URL.

The only multi-domain spiritual intelligence API. 9 domains (astrology, tarot, numerology, crystals, dreams, I-Ching, angel numbers, Vedic astrology, location), 122+ endpoints, one API key, instant activation. Native MCP support for AI agents.

## Who uses RoxyAPI

- **Developers** building astrology apps, tarot platforms, numerology calculators, or dream journals
- **AI agent builders** connecting Claude, GPT, or Gemini to real calculation engines via MCP
- **Vibe coders** shipping insight apps with Cursor, Bolt, or Replit using zero domain knowledge
- **Founders and brands** launching branded spiritual experiences for their audience

## Quick start (60 seconds)

**1. Get your API key** at [roxyapi.com/pricing](https://roxyapi.com/pricing). Instant delivery, no account required.

**2. Make your first call:**
```bash
curl -H "X-API-Key: YOUR_KEY" https://roxyapi.com/api/v2/tarot/draw -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"count": 3}'
```

**3. Monitor usage:**
```bash
curl -H "X-API-Key: YOUR_KEY" https://roxyapi.com/api/v2/usage
```

## AI agent integration (MCP)

RoxyAPI has native Model Context Protocol support. Your AI agent auto-discovers all 122+ endpoints as callable tools with zero configuration:
- **Claude Desktop, Cursor, Windsurf**: Add MCP server URL in settings
- **OpenAI Agents, Gemini ADK**: Connect via Streamable HTTP transport
- **Custom agents**: Use the MCP Python/TypeScript SDK

MCP endpoints: `https://roxyapi.com/mcp/{domain}` (e.g., `/mcp/astrology-api`, `/mcp/tarot-api`)

Setup guide: [roxyapi.com/docs/mcp](https://roxyapi.com/docs/mcp)

## Authentication

All endpoints require an API key via header or query param:
- **Header (recommended):** `X-API-Key: YOUR_KEY`
- **Query param (testing):** `?api_key=YOUR_KEY`

## Response format

Clean JSON, no wrapper objects. Errors return `{ "error": "message", "code": "error_code" }`. The `error` field is human-readable (may change wording). The `code` field is machine-readable (stable — safe to switch on programmatically).

Rate limit headers on every response: `X-RateLimit-Limit`, `X-RateLimit-Remaining`, `X-RateLimit-Used`

## Errors

All errors return `{ "error": "message", "code": "error_code" }`:

| Status | Code | When |
|--------|------|------|
| 400 | `validation_error` | Missing or invalid parameters |
| 401 | `api_key_required` | No API key provided |
| 401 | `invalid_api_key` | Key format invalid or tampered |
| 401 | `subscription_not_found` | Key references non-existent subscription |
| 401 | `subscription_inactive` | Subscription cancelled, expired, or suspended |
| 404 | `not_found` | Resource not found |
| 429 | `rate_limit_exceeded` | Monthly quota reached |
| 500 | `internal_error` | Server error |

## Pricing

Flat per-request pricing. Every call counts the same, whether a planet position or a full birth chart with aspects. No credit systems, no variable costs. Plans from $39 to $699/month.

See [roxyapi.com/pricing](https://roxyapi.com/pricing)

## Resources

- [Quickstart guide](https://roxyapi.com/docs/quickstart) - first API call in 60 seconds
- [Documentation](https://roxyapi.com/docs) - guides, tutorials, domain reference
- [MCP setup](https://roxyapi.com/docs/mcp) - connect AI agents
- [Starter apps](https://roxyapi.com/starters) - clone and deploy in 30 minutes
- [FAQ](https://roxyapi.com/faq) - common questions
- [Contact](https://roxyapi.com/contact) - support and API key recovery

tags.name: I-Ching
tags.description: I-Ching oracle API + MCP for AI agents and developers. Access 3,000 years of Chinese wisdom with all 64 hexagrams, 384 changing lines, 8 trigrams, and modern interpretations for love, career, and decision-making. Cast readings with the authentic three-coin method, get daily hexagrams, and explore the Book of Changes programmatically. 9+ endpoints optimized for divination apps, AI oracle chatbots, fortune-telling platforms, daily wisdom features, and spiritual guidance products.

apiKey.name: X-API-Key
apiKey.description: Your API key for accessing RoxyAPI. Alternatively, you can pass the API key as a query parameter "api_key".

number.description: Hexagram number in King Wen sequence (1-64).

chinese.description: Original Chinese name of the hexagram.

english.description: English translation of the hexagram name.

pinyin.description: Pinyin romanization of the Chinese name with tone marks.

upperTrigram.description: Upper trigram (lines 4-6). One of 8 trigrams: Heaven, Earth, Thunder, Wind, Water, Fire, Mountain, Lake.

lowerTrigram.description: Lower trigram (lines 1-3). Combines with upper trigram to form the hexagram.

number.description: Hexagram number in the traditional King Wen sequence (1-64), the standard ordering used in I-Ching divination for over 3,000 years.

symbol.description: Unicode hexagram symbol (U+4DC0 block) representing all six lines. Use for visual display in I-Ching apps and divination interfaces.

chinese.description: Original Chinese character name of the hexagram in traditional script.

english.description: English translation of the hexagram name, conveying the core concept and life situation it represents.

pinyin.description: Pinyin romanization of the Chinese name with tone marks for correct pronunciation.

binary.description: Binary line pattern (6 digits, bottom to top). 1 = yang (solid line), 0 = yin (broken line). Lines 1-3 form the lower trigram, lines 4-6 form the upper trigram.

upperTrigram.description: Upper trigram (lines 4-6). One of 8 trigrams: Heaven, Earth, Thunder, Wind, Water, Fire, Mountain, Lake.

lowerTrigram.description: Lower trigram (lines 1-3). Combines with the upper trigram to form the hexagram and its meaning.

judgment.example: The Creative works sublime success, furthering through perseverance.
judgment.description: The Judgment (Tuan) text, the primary oracle statement of the hexagram offering core guidance and outcome.

image.example: The movement of heaven is full of power. Thus the superior man makes himself strong and untiring.
image.description: The Image (Xiang) text, symbolic guidance derived from the trigram combination describing the ideal attitude and action.

changingLines.description: Changing line interpretations for all 6 lines

general.example: This hexagram represents the primal power of creation and the energy of pure initiative.
general.description: General life situation interpretation of this hexagram.

love.example: A time of strong attraction and passionate connection. Take the initiative in expressing your feelings.
love.description: Love and relationship guidance from this hexagram.

career.example: Exceptional opportunities for advancement and recognition. Your creative ideas are at their peak.

decision.example: The time is right for bold action. Trust your instincts and move forward with confidence.
decision.description: Decision-making guidance for whether to act, wait, retreat, or advance based on this hexagram.

advice.example: Be like the heavens: consistent, powerful, and untiring. Make yourself strong through steady effort.
advice.description: Practical wisdom and actionable advice from this hexagram for daily life application.

position.description: Line position (1-6, bottom to top). In I-Ching, each hexagram has six lines (yao) read from bottom upward.

text.description: Changing line interpretation text. Applies only when this specific line is a changing line (old yin or old yang) in a casting.

number.description: Trigram number (1-8) in the traditional I-Ching ordering.

symbol.description: Unicode trigram symbol (three lines) for visual display in I-Ching interfaces and Bagua diagrams.

chinese.description: Original Chinese character name of the trigram in traditional script.

english.description: English name representing the natural force or element this trigram embodies.

pinyin.description: Pinyin romanization of the Chinese name with tone marks for correct pronunciation.

binary.description: Three-digit binary representation of the trigram lines (bottom to top). 1 = yang (solid), 0 = yin (broken).

element.description: Five element (Wu Xing) correspondence: Metal, Wood, Water, Fire, or Earth. Used in Chinese metaphysics and feng shui analysis.

attribute.description: Core attribute or quality this trigram represents in I-Ching philosophy (e.g., Creative, Receptive, Arousing).

familyMember.description: Family archetype in the Bagua system. Each trigram corresponds to a family role (Father, Mother, First Son, First Daughter, etc.).

direction.description: Compass direction in the King Wen (Later Heaven) Bagua arrangement. Used in feng shui spatial analysis.

bodyPart.description: Body part associated with this trigram in traditional Chinese medicine and I-Ching body mapping.

animal.description: Animal symbol associated with this trigram in classical I-Ching imagery and divination.

season.description: Season or time period associated with this trigram in the annual cycle of Chinese cosmology.

quality.description: Energetic quality describing the dynamic nature of this trigram (e.g., Strong, Devoted, Joyous, Gentle).

meaning.example: The Creative force representing pure yang energy, strength, leadership, and the power of initiative.
meaning.description: Concise interpretation of the trigram covering its symbolic meaning, key associations, and guidance for understanding hexagrams containing this trigram.

post.description: Receive a daily I-Ching hexagram for guidance and reflection. This endpoint uses seeded randomness to ensure the same seed gets the same hexagram on the same day - perfect for "Hexagram of the Day" features in oracle apps, meditation platforms, and daily wisdom tools. Returns the hexagram with judgment, image, and interpretations for love, career, decisions, and practical advice based on ancient Chinese wisdom from the Book of Changes.

schema.description: Response language (ISO 639-1). Supported: en, tr, de, es, hi, pt, fr, ru. Defaults to en. Languages without translations yet return English.

parameters.description: Response language (ISO 639-1). Supported: en, tr, de, es, hi, pt, fr, ru. Defaults to en. Languages without translations yet return English.
parameters.name: lang

seed.description: Optional seed for reproducible readings. Same seed + same date = same hexagram every time. Pass any unique identifier (userId, email hash, session token). Omit for anonymous daily readings.

date.description: Date for the reading in YYYY-MM-DD format. Defaults to today (UTC). Useful for viewing past daily readings or pre-generating future ones.

200.description: Daily hexagram reading with full interpretation

date.description: Date this daily hexagram is for (YYYY-MM-DD, UTC).

seed.description: Computed seed used for this reading. Same seed always produces the same hexagram.

number.description: Hexagram number in King Wen sequence (1-64).

english.description: English translation of the hexagram name.

judgment.description: The Judgment (Tuan) text, the primary oracle statement of the hexagram offering core guidance.

image.description: The Image (Xiang) text, symbolic guidance derived from the trigram combination describing the ideal action.

decision.description: Decision-making guidance for whether to act, wait, or change course.

interpretation.description: Modern interpretations across life areas based on ancient I-Ching wisdom.

dailyMessage.example: Your hexagram for 2026-01-28: The Creative (乾). Supreme success through perseverance.
dailyMessage.description: Concise daily message summarizing the hexagram guidance

error.description: Human-readable error message. May change wording.

code.description: Machine-readable error code. Stable identifier for programmatic error handling.

post.summary: Cast daily I-Ching reading with changing lines
post.description: Cast a complete daily I-Ching reading using the traditional three-coin method with seeded randomness. Unlike the simple daily hexagram, this provides the full casting experience with line values (6-9), changing line positions, and the resulting hexagram if transformation occurs. Same seed + same date = same casting result. Perfect for I-Ching divination apps requiring authentic oracle experience with daily consistency.

get.description: Browse all 64 I-Ching hexagrams from the Book of Changes with their Chinese names, English translations, and trigram compositions. The hexagrams are ordered by the traditional King Wen sequence used in I-Ching divination for 3,000 years. Each hexagram represents a unique life situation combining two trigrams (Heaven, Earth, Thunder, Wind, Water, Fire, Mountain, Lake) into profound wisdom for decision-making and self-understanding.

get.description: Find an I-Ching hexagram by its binary line pattern. Provide 6 digits (0 or 1) representing broken (yin) and solid (yang) lines from bottom to top. Use this for custom divination interfaces where users input their own line configurations, or to find hexagrams matching specific trigram combinations. Example: "111111" returns Hexagram 1 (The Creative), "000000" returns Hexagram 2 (The Receptive).

Returns the full hexagram with Chinese name, English translation, judgment text, image text, modern interpretations for general situations, love, career, and decision-making, plus all six changing line meanings. Use this to display detailed hexagram information after casting or for educational reference.

get.description: Generate an authentic I-Ching reading using the traditional three-coin casting method. Each of the six lines is determined by virtually tossing three coins, producing values 6-9 where 6 (old yin) and 9 (old yang) are changing lines. Returns the primary hexagram with full interpretation, the line values showing which lines are changing, and if any lines change, the resulting hexagram that the primary transforms into. Optionally provide a seed for reproducible castings. Perfect for divination apps, oracle features, and decision-making tools.

parameters.name: seed

200.description: Complete I-Ching reading with primary and resulting hexagrams.

lines.description: Line values (6-9) from bottom to top. 6=old yin (changing), 7=young yang, 8=young yin, 9=old yang (changing)

changingLinePositions.description: Positions of changing lines (1-6, bottom to top)

allOf.description: Hexagram the primary transforms into after changing lines (present only if there are changing lines)

get.description: Retrieve all 8 I-Ching trigrams (bagua) - the fundamental building blocks of hexagrams. Each trigram consists of three lines and represents a primal force of nature: Heaven (Qian), Earth (Kun), Thunder (Zhen), Wind (Xun), Water (Kan), Fire (Li), Mountain (Gen), and Lake (Dui). Understanding trigrams is essential for interpreting hexagram meanings, as each hexagram combines an upper and lower trigram.
